response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack software developer with strong web, testing, and craftsmanship credentials, but the role demands deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. His low-latency DLang systems work and C/C++ experience at National Instruments show systems aptitude, but there is no evidence of computer architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, or performance modeling at the silicon level. This is a fundamental domain mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Strong systems programming exposure: low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests/second with async I/O and fibers
Broad, senior-level engineering experience (18+ years) spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Demonstrated technical leadership: open-source authorship, workshops, and TDD/craftsmanship advocacy
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or AI accelerator workload background
Career trajectory is web/application-focused, far from silicon performance architecture at principal level
